Removed per WP:NPOV and WP:UNDUE—10-year cost cost analysis misrepresents a 40–60 year reactor lifespan and excludes strategic and environmental context. Also no source for enrichment claim.

Undue weight given to an economist editorial. The IAEA has also not 'refuted' that it's used for peaceful purposes, only that Tehran has not provided adequate explanations for why this level of enrichment is required for non-military use. There's a distinction there..

Reverted 1 edit by [[Special:Contributions/NicolasCaous|NicolasCaous]] ([[User talk:NicolasCaous|talk]]): Please discuss on talk then, which is [[WP:BRD|proper procedure when edits are challenged]]; and please be civil

Don't give me this bullshit. Do you use "clandestinely" when you want to say "secretly"? The dictionary is pretty explicit: "especially describing something that is not officially allowed". If you want that to be the meaning, then put "secretly" instead of the biased term "clandestinely", since that implies it is not allowed. I'm keeping my change as this is according to Wikipedia's policy of WP:NPOV
Reverted 1 edit by [[Special:Contributions/84.106.166.184|84.106.166.184]] ([[User talk:84.106.166.184|talk]]): Clandestine does not imply illegal, just secret, trying to conceal it

Since Iran is a sovereign nation, it doesn't make sense to say its nuclear program is "clandestine", as having a nuclear program is not ilegal neither in Iranian law nor in international law. If that is the case, every nuclear program from any country would be "clandestine".
